items.text:
I'm past that golden platform,
and back to making my way
past silver barriers.

Based on what I've now seen, it does make sense
that the silver and gold are themselves

a nonverbal message, a way of drawing the attention
towards certain things.

But I'm not sure why they use the more-precious metal
for the more-common gateways and object securements,

and the less-precious metal for this big destination
platform.

and the larger waypoints in these incremental journeys,
whlie also valuable, are somewhat less to be fixated upon,
thus, gold.

Or -- all right, I realize I am being stupid here.
If water can behave so differently between worlds,
then surely metals can, as well.
